central_thesis: The market is demonstrating resilience and identifying new growth drivers, particularly in AI-related technologies, memory, and specific regional market opportunities, despite geopolitical tensions and some sector-specific headwinds. Strategic investments, product innovation, and strong financial outlooks for key players are expected to drive future performance.

sub_claims.claim: Broader equity markets are showing resilience despite geopolitical concerns.

evidence_list.content: US equities shrugged off early losses following the weekend's attacks on Iran, rebounding sharply off early session lows to close the day in the green - SPX +4bps to 6881 (4/11 sectors +ve, Energy +195bps, Consumer Staples -135bps), NDX +13bps to 24,992, RTY +90bps to 2655.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: High, as it provides specific index movements and sector performance data.

sub_claims.claim: There is strong growth and innovation in AI and related semiconductor/optical components.

evidence_list.content: DATA CENTER OPTICAL STOCKS ripped yesterday after Nvidia announced it will invest $2bn apiece in Lumentum (stock +7%) and Coherent (+13%). Deals include purchase agreements and access rights for advanced laser components and are intended to support research, development, and the expansion of US-based manufacturing capabilities for advanced optics, which are critical for high-speed data transmission in AI data centers. The investments will support R&D.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: High, as it's a direct announcement of significant investment from a major industry player (Nvidia) with specific financial figures and stock reactions.

evidence_list.content: In Europe stocks with optical exposure include AIXA GY (30% of sales), HLMA (20%), BESI (10%) and Soitec (5%). Nokia and Huber + Suhner are beneficiaries to a lesser extent.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: Medium, as it's an assessment of beneficiaries based on sales exposure, which is a reasonable proxy for impact.

evidence_list.content: NVDA GTC will help drive AI memory momentum.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: Medium, as it's a forward-looking statement from an industry observer, likely based on NVIDIA's influence.

evidence_list.content: NVIDIA is poised to unveil its next-generation AI chips, including the "Feynman" architecture, at GTC 2026 this month, with Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix expected to continue their high-bandwidth memory (HBM) collaboration.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: Medium, based on industry expectations and existing partnerships.

evidence_list.content: SAP board restructure – sees CEO Christian Klein focusing more on A&I & R&D.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: High, as it's a factual statement about a company's internal strategic shift.

evidence_list.content: Disruptive Tech Conference here in London today and tomorrow -register here. To me this a chance to see AI startups who potentially are threats to listed counterparts or takeover targets.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: Medium, as it highlights a relevant industry event and its potential for identifying future growth.

sub_claims.claim: Specific companies across various sectors are demonstrating positive outlooks and strategic advancements.

evidence_list.content: Lottomatica: Results in-line; FY26 EBITDA guide slightly ahead at the midpoint; requesting buyback authorisation for up to 12.5% of share capital. Should be positive today. LTMC: Q a touch better and guide slightly ahead of street. Q4 revenues inline, EBIT 4% ahead, DPS 7% ahead. Its given FY 26 guidance of revenue EU2.39 to EU2.46nm vs street at EU2.44 and adjusted EBITDA EU940 to EU980m vs. street EU954m. Lottomatica’s market share was strong in iGaming at 31.9% (vs. 3Q25: 30.6%), but was lower yoy and qoq for iSports at 31.6% (vs. 3Q25: 32.7%). Total Online market share was up +0.4pp yoy, supported by new Online concessions in Italy coming into effect in mid-November, which have led to share losses for tail operators. The company also note that PWO market share improved to 6.3% in 4Q25 and management believe this is on track for sustainable growth post the migration.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: High, based on reported financial results, guidance, and market share data.

evidence_list.content: MU preview- Jim expects another strong quarter driven by pricing upside, with HBM4 ramp in focus. He expects Micron to deliver ~4% upside to Street revenue in the quarter and guide to strong QoQ revenue growth in the May quarter, driven almost entirely by pricing upside... expect revenue/GM/EPS of $19.7 bn/71.0%/$9.33 for the quarter vs. the Street at $19.0 bn/68.6%/$8.59. For May quarter guidance expect revenue/GM/EPS of $23.5 bn/74.8%/$11.95 compared with the Street at $21.6 bn/70.9%/$10.08. For CY26, our revenue/EPS estimates are 5%/10% above Street consensus.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: Medium-High, as it comes from an analyst ('Jim') with specific numerical forecasts that are above consensus.

evidence_list.content: Apple: Per GIR announced the iPhone 17e and M4 iPad Airs, where the most prominent surprises include (a) stable starting price on the iPhone 17e, and (b) 256 GB starting storage for iPhone 17e (v. 128 GB starting iPhone 16e storage). AAPL effectively reduced prices on the iPhone 17e through (a) holding starting iPhone 17e prices flat at $599 (256 GB iPhone 16e models started at $699 last year), and (b) 512 GB iPhone 17e now starting at $799 (v. $899 512 GB iPhone 16e starting price). We believe updates to iPhone 17e and M4 iPad Air should support AAPL's ability to continue growing market share across consumer electronics, particularly amidst industry-wide memory cost pressure.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: High for product details and pricing, medium for market share forecast (as it's a belief).

evidence_list.content: VAT FY26 guidance expects orders, sales, EBITDA, EBITDA margin, net income and FCF to be higher than in 2025. VAT book-to-bill ratio is expected to be substantially above 1 (consensus at 1.05x).
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: High, as it's direct company guidance on future performance and a key industry metric.

evidence_list.content: Taiwanese matured foundries Vanguard (5347) and PSMC (6770) are expected to see spillover effects, +VE for short term momentum.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: Medium, as it's an analyst's expectation based on a competitor's constraints.

evidence_list.content: Vodafone has partnered with Amazon Leo to expand 4G and 5G mobile coverage in remote areas across Europe and Africa. The partnership aims to facilitate easier and cheaper setup of base stations, avoiding costly fiber or fixed wireless links. Amazon Leo's service will also enhance network resilience for emergency and critical online services by providing backup if fiber links are damaged. The rollout will begin in Germany and other European countries, followed by expansion across Africa via Vodacom, with the first mobile sites expected to be connected later this year.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: High, as it's a factual announcement of a strategic partnership and its intended benefits.

evidence_list.content: Scout24 closes acquisition of Fotocasa, Habitaclia. Fotocasa is the No. 2 real estate marketplace in Spain, behind Idealista. As part of the deal, Scout24 has also acquired Habitaclia, a regional property site focused on Catalonia and the Balearic Islands
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: High, as it's a factual announcement of a completed acquisition strengthening market position.

evidence_list.content: Draftkings GIR attended DraftKings’ Investor Day 2026, during which the company outlined a forward strategy around several key pillars: a) the announcement of a new super-app strategy centered around DraftKings Sports & Casino; b) highlighted its competitive advantage across product, technology, trust & marketing; c) outlined the prediction markets opportunity (significant TAM expansion + positive/attractive unit economics); & d) provided an updated long-term financial & capital allocation framework.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: High, as it reports on a company's official investor day presentation of its strategy and outlook.

sub_claims.claim: The Software sector is showing signs of recovery.

evidence_list.content: While Software has started to bounce off the lows (Software has o/p the NDX by ~550 bps over 5 days, quietly its best run in >12-months)
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: High, as it provides specific performance metrics (outperformance against NDX, duration of run).

sub_claims.claim: Anthropic's Claude AI is maintaining a strong market position and innovating.

evidence_list.content: Anthropic says Claude had "elevated errors" on Monday, though holding at top spot in App Store
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: High, as it's a verifiable market position.

evidence_list.content: Plus Anthropic Tries to Win Users From ChatGPT With Memory Feature for free users.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: High, as it's a factual statement about a new product feature.
